D4 Colorimetry L6 Create Reference

An OKLCH Palette

fix lightness and chroma, step the hue in equal angles, and build a palette whose swatches feel evenly spaced and evenly bright

OKLCH separates lightness (L), colourfulness (C), and hue (H). Hold L and C, step H in equal angles, and the swatches read as equally light and equally spaced — the promise a uniform palette needs.

Set the lightness and chroma shared by every swatch, then rotate the starting hue. Twelve hues are stepped 30° apart. Any swatch that leaves the sRGB gamut at this L and C is struck through — push chroma down until the ring is solid.
